message.content: Adversaries may exploit authentication certificates by stealing or forging them to gain unauthorized access to remote systems or resources. These certificates, used for signing, encrypting, and authenticating, can be compromised from encrypted storage, unsecured files, or directly from the Windows certificate store. Attackers may also abuse certificate enrollment processes to request or renew certificates with elevated permissions. Misconfigurations in certificate settings can lead to privilege escalation, allowing attackers to impersonate privileged accounts or maintain persistence despite password changes. Tools like AADInternals and Mimikatz facilitate the creation and export of these certificates. Notably, APT29 has exploited misconfigured AD CS templates to impersonate admin users. Mitigation strategies include securing certificate storage, monitoring certificate issuance and usage, and ensuring proper configuration of certificate templates and permissions.
